rationale: control_dependence: fixed_or_predefined -- C04-P23 ('In autonomous mode, there is no human involvement other than providing the initial research idea ... Each subtask moves on to the next subtask sequentially upon completion'); the top-level subtask progression is unconditional on subtask quality (explicitly contrasted with the evaluation-gated co-pilot checkpoint in C04-P24, where 'a human is involved in reviewing the work' and can 'ask the agent to repeat the subtask'), so the source CONFIRMS outcome-independence at that level -- this is fixed_or_predefined, not not_reported (the source states the independence rather than being silent). failure -- C04-P08 (data-preparation code is 'iteratively executed until the code is bug-free'), C04-P11 ('the agent attempts to repair the code for N_rep tries' on compile failure), C04-P14 (mle-solver self-reflection on 'how to fix this issue'), C04-P29/P30/P31 (observed reactive fixes/guards). score -- C04-P11/P12/P13 (mle-solver's scoring function returns a score and 'the list of top programs is updated if the score is higher than the existing programs'), C04-P15 (top-scoring programs retained/sampled; 'the top modification' kept and 'the lowest-scoring program' replaced), C04-P22 ('Based on these scores, the PhD agent then decides whether to finalize the project or revisit earlier subtasks'), C04-P35 (professor-agent reward-model scores plan/code/output alignment '0 to 1 as a float'). observation_result -- C04-P16 (the PhD/Postdoc agents interpret mle-solver's experimental results, forming 'the basis for the report writing phase'). selection_signal names these drivers, with the fixed autonomous-mode progression listed as its own signal. selection_objective: local_repair -- the compile/repair loops (C04-P08/P11/P14/P29/P30/P31). performance_improvement -- C04-P12/P13 (the scoring function measures how closely code/output match 'the planning goals'), C04-P35 (reward-model alignment score). The former 'exploration' label moves to generation_scope (below): the diversity in C04 is maintained inside the candidate pool, not pursued as a selection purpose, so no diversity_directed_selection at the pi level; no uncertainty_reduction or hypothesis_model_discrimination is reported. generation_scope: candidate_diversification -- C04-P15 ('In top program sampling, a collection of the highest-scoring programs is maintained, and one program is randomly sampled before executing a command, ensuring diversity while retaining quality'); the diversity is a property maintained in the generated candidate collection. candidate_selection_rule: top_k_ranked -- mle-solver keeps a ranked top-programs list and replaces the lowest-scoring program with 'the top modification' (C04-P11/P12/P15); sampled_subset -- 'one program is randomly sampled' from the top collection before execution (C04-P15). candidate_execution_rule: batch -- 'For batch-parallelization, each solver step involves making N modifications simultaneously' (C04-P15); until_success -- data-prep code is run 'iteratively ... until the code is bug-free' (C04-P08); until_budget -- 'the agent attempts to repair the code for N_rep tries (N_rep=3 in our experiments) before returning an error and moving on' (C04-P11), and the paper-solver search is capped ('A limit of 5 was placed', C04-P31). No objective label appears in any rule. formal_epistemic_utility_evidence: not_reported -- the mle-solver and reward-model scores are alignment/quality scores, not a Bayesian model-discrimination objective; no prior/posterior, likelihood, or EIG/epistemic-utility term is stated (corpus-level footnote).
